questions.statement:
<p>From the given V - I graphs for three resistors R<sub>1</sub>, R<sub>2</sub> and R<sub>3</sub> it may be concluded that</p>

<p><img alt="" src="//storage.googleapis.com/tb-img/production/23/01/F1_Vinanti_Teaching_24.01.23_D6.png" style="width: 201px; height: 152px;" /></p>

options: The resistance of the device increases when the current increases. /
The resistance of the device remains constant when the p.d. increases. /
The resistance of the device remains constant when the current increases. /

questions.statement: The resistance of a metallic wire of length 1m and area of cross-section 1sq.metre having a resistivity is&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(2 \times 10^{-6} \)</span>&nbsp;ohm -m is:
Which of the following graphs correctly represents the variation of heat energy (U) produced in a metallic conductor in a given time as a function of potential difference (V) across the conductor?
Choose the CORRECT statement regarding Ohm&rsquo;s law<strong>:</strong>
options: It is valid for any circumstances, i.e. independent of temperature /
Ohm&rsquo;s law is valid for all conducting materials /
The temperature must be constant for validation of this law /

questions.statement:
<p>Assertion: The 200 W bulbs glows with more brightness then 100 W bulbs.</p>
Reason: A 100 W bulb has more resistance than a 200 W bulb.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and the reason is the correct explanation of the assertion. /
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion. /
If assertion is true but reason is false. /
If assertion is false but reason is true. /

questions.statement: An electron is allowed to rotate on a metallic circle of the perimeter of&nbsp; 20&nbsp;&pi; made from copper wire of resistance&nbsp; 0.0171&nbsp;&Omega; per unit length and rotates with speed 15 m/s, what will be the potential difference per unit length of the circle
